mission.title: dapr: State Store Query API
mission.description: State Store Query API. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
## In what area(s)?

## Describe the proposal
This proposal is a follow-up on #1339 

This is a proposed design of the API for querying the **state store**.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "State Store Query API" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I actually do use parametrized query in `CosmosDB` implementation of the visitor interface.
Take a look at the function `setNextParameter`:

```go
func (q *Query) setNextParameter(val string) string {
	pname := fmt.Sprintf("%d__", len(q.query.Parameters))
	q.query.Parameters = append(q.query.Parameters, documentdb.Parameter{Name: pname, Value: val})

	return pname
}
```
